questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a logical clock in distributed systems?
options: A) To measure physical time
B) To synchronize clocks across systems
C) To manage process states
D) To handle network latency
questions.explanation: A logical clock is used to synchronize clocks across systems, allowing them to agree on the order of events and ensure consistency in distributed systems.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a logical clock?
options: A) It can be set to a specific value
B) It can be incremented and decremented
C) It is always synchronized with physical time
D) It is used to store process states
questions.explanation: A logical clock can be incremented and decremented to represent the passage of time in a distributed system, allowing processes to agree on the order of events.

questions.question: What is the difference between a logical clock and a physical clock?
options: A) A physical clock measures time more accurately
B) A logical clock is synchronized with physical time
C) A logical clock represents the order of events
D) A physical clock is used to manage process states
questions.explanation: A physical clock measures time in the real world, while a logical clock represents the order of events in a distributed system, allowing processes to agree on the sequence of events.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using logical clocks in distributed systems?
options: A) Improved accuracy
B) Increased consistency
C) Reduced latency
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Using logical clocks in distributed systems can improve consistency, reduce latency, and provide a more accurate representation of the order of events.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a clock vector in logical clocks?
options: A) To represent the current time
B) To synchronize clocks across systems
C) To manage process states
D) To represent the relative order of events
questions.explanation: A clock vector is used to represent the relative order of events in a distributed system, allowing processes to agree on the sequence of events and synchronize their clocks.
